constructs: goal activation, consciousness, behavior control, persistence
claim: Mental goal representations activated outside awareness operate as effectively as conscious goals to guide behavior.
The action-regret relationship flips when prior negative outcomes make action the norm rather than exception.
High self-esteem individuals subjectively distance themselves from failure and keep success psychologically close independent of actual time.
Relationship commitment transforms vengeful impulses into pro-relationship motivation enabling forgiveness of betrayal.

constructs: implicit stereotypes, self-identity, group membership, consistency
claim: Implicit math-male stereotypes create self-math associations that are constrained for women but enhanced for men through cognitive consistency.

phenomenon: adaptive failure and negative emotionality escalation
constructs: childhood adaptation, personality continuity, negative emotion, development
claim: Poor childhood achievement and conduct problems predict increasing negative emotionality by adulthood beyond trait continuity.
